The main claim is: ARC is offering $5k bounties for two concrete near-linear-time matrix-completion problems (testing for a PSD completion, and fast approximate squaring) that arose in its research.

top_comment.author: Dagon
top_comment.text: Like all Pascal-wager-like arguments, it's extremely sensitive to relative sizes of low-evidence very-small-absolute-magnitude probability estimates.  My expectation is that it will always be an error to give significant weight to truly acausal (as opposed to distantly-causal) considerations in real actions in our universe.
